message.abstract:
<jats:title>ABSTRACT</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Introduction</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Coronaviruses typically cause influenza like illness which progresses to ARDS along with other systemic manifestations. India is experiencing its second wave with a huge surge in the number of cases exponentially causing huge impact on health care infrastructure and the demand supply chain. As a result several new modalities have been used, one of which is the use of remdesivir and baricitinib . Hence this study is aimed at finding out the clinical and biochemical profile of the patients who have received the combination</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Materials and Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>All the patients who have received the combination meeting the inclusion and exclusion criteria have been included in the study. A total of 31 participants were included and their records were retrospectively analyzed.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>There was a significant reduction in the oxygen requirement, CRP and IL-6 levels with p values<0.05. However, in the non-survivors group, there was no statistically significant reduction. Serial monitoring of NLR ratio showed increase towards the fifth day, especially in the non-survivor group it was as high as 41.24. The mortality rate was found to be 10% and the cause being secondary sepsis in all of them.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusion</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The ACTT-2 trail has proved the efficacy of the use of the remdesivir and baricitinib combination with mortality benefit. In our study we found similar results which was well co-related with clinical and biochemical parameters like CRP and IL-6 especially in people with co-morbidites.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: THE EFFECT OF BARICITINIB USAGE ON THE CLINICAL AND BIOCHEMICAL PROFILES OF COVID-19 PATIENTS- A RETROSPECTIVE OBSERVATIONAL STUDY
